steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence uses 'while' to join two clauses. The second clause — 'students of traditional methods often only ______ such songs at the age of five' — needs a finite verb to function as an independent clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'begin learning': 'begin' is a finite present-tense verb; it can serve as the main verb of the clause. ✓
  • Choice B — 'beginning to learn': 'beginning' is a present participle — non-finite. Without an auxiliary, it cannot anchor an independent clause. ✗
  • Choice C — 'to begin learning': an infinitive phrase — non-finite. Cannot serve as the main verb of a clause. ✗
  • Choice D — 'having begun to learn': a perfect participle — non-finite. Cannot anchor an independent clause on its own. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
